channel.title: University Record — Fitzherbert University
channel.description: Original scholarship from the faculties, centres, and institutes of Fitzherbert University — advancing the discourse on institutional governance, competitive strategy, and technological sovereignty.
channel.copyright: © 2026 Fitzherbert University. Established 1783. All rights reserved.

item.title: Constitutional Precedent in Digital Governance
item.description: The principles embedded in Fitzherbert University's 1783 Charter — separation of powers, transparency mandates, and procedural due process — provide a surprisingly robust framework for governing digital systems.
item.author: Professor Victoria Langford

item.title: The Future of AI-Governed Institutions
item.description: As artificial intelligence systems assume greater roles in institutional decision-making, universities must develop constitutional frameworks that preserve academic sovereignty while enabling responsible automation.
item.author: Professor Margaret Sinclair

item.title: Fiduciary Duty in Perpetuity
item.description: Managing a $14.2 billion endowment with an infinite time horizon imposes unique fiduciary obligations — obligations that extend beyond financial returns to encompass ethical stewardship and intergenerational equity.
item.author: Professor Richard Pemberton

item.title: Agentic RAG for Institutional Memory
item.description: Retrieval-augmented generation, deployed within a governed agent framework, enables institutions to build compounding knowledge systems — living memories that grow more authoritative with every interaction.
item.author: Professor Margaret Sinclair

item.title: Game Theory and Competitive Strategy in Collegiate Systems
item.description: Collegiate athletics operates within a complex strategic landscape where game-theoretic principles — dominant strategies, Nash equilibria, and information asymmetry — determine competitive advantage as decisively as raw athletic talent.
item.author: Professor Richard Pemberton

item.title: Simulation-Driven Recruitment Strategy
item.description: Monte Carlo simulation enables athletics programmes to model thousands of potential recruitment scenarios, quantifying risk and optimising scholarship allocation under uncertainty.
item.author: Professor James Harrington

item.title: Opponent Modeling in Modern Esports
item.description: Esports competitors generate thousands of observable decisions per match. Systematic opponent modelling — using Bayesian inference, behavioural clustering, and real-time adaptation — transforms this data into decisive strategic advantage.
item.author: Professor James Harrington

item.title: Endowment Stewardship in the Age of Volatility
item.description: With $14.2 billion under stewardship, Fitzherbert University's endowment represents a covenant between generations. This article examines how institutional investors maintain disciplined strategies across centuries of market uncertainty.
item.author: The Editorial Board

item.title: Knowledge Graphs for Institutional Intelligence
item.description: Knowledge graphs provide the semantic infrastructure that connects institutional data — research outputs, governance decisions, financial records, and personnel — into a queryable, machine-readable intelligence layer.
item.author: Professor Margaret Sinclair

item.title: The Ethics of Predictive Analytics in Admissions
item.description: Universities increasingly deploy predictive analytics in admissions. This article examines the ethical frameworks necessary to ensure that algorithmic efficiency does not compromise fairness, diversity, or the holistic evaluation of human potential.
item.author: Professor Victoria Langford

item.title: Biomechanical Modelling for Injury Prevention
item.description: Finite element analysis and musculoskeletal simulation enable sports medicine teams to predict injury risk, optimise training loads, and design personalised prevention programmes for collegiate athletes.
item.author: Professor James Harrington

item.title: Academic Freedom in the Age of Algorithms
item.description: As AI systems increasingly mediate access to knowledge — through search ranking, content recommendation, and information filtering — universities must actively defend academic freedom against algorithmic constraints on inquiry.
item.author: Professor Victoria Langford

item.title: Transfer Portal Economics
item.description: The transfer portal has transformed collegiate athletics into a functioning labour market with pricing signals, information asymmetry, and strategic arbitrage opportunities — demanding economic literacy from coaching staffs.
item.author: Professor Richard Pemberton

item.title: Vector Databases for Institutional Memory
item.description: Vector databases transform institutional documents — governance records, research papers, policy documents — into high-dimensional embeddings that enable semantic search, intelligent retrieval, and knowledge discovery across centuries of institutional output.
item.author: Professor Margaret Sinclair

item.title: Institutional Resilience Through Governance Design
item.description: Institutional resilience is not accidental. It is the product of governance design — constitutional structures that enable adaptation while preserving identity, balancing innovation with continuity across centuries of environmental change.
item.author: The Editorial Board
